title: Achoriphragma schugnanum
taxonomy: Achoriphragma schugnanum is a species of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae, commonly known as mustard or cabbage family. It is native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia. The plant belongs to the genus Achoriphragma. The classification of Achoriphragma schugnanum includes its placement within the family Brassicaceae and its taxonomic ranking as a species. Further details about its classification may require specific research on botanical classification systems.

intro: Achoriphragma schugnanum, also known as Schugnanum or Schugnan sedge, is a type of plant belonging to the family Cyperaceae. This plant is valued for its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate respiratory issues and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Achoriphragma schugnanum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, herbal preparations made from this plant include infusions and decoctions, often used to treat respiratory conditions such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as to boost the immune system.
